_dryrun_note: Phase 1 bottleneck identification on RLHF reward model overoptimization. Reads phase0/lit_table.md + lit_results.json. Single LLM call.

intake.expertise: RLHF training (PPO + DPO familiarity) + theoretical preference learning
intake.baseline: vanilla PPO with KL-to-SFT regularization (constant β)
intake.limitation: policy KL stays small but proxy reward keeps climbing while held-out true reward (gold model) plateaus and degrades — classical Goodhart's-law overoptimization signature; current heuristic mitigations (early stopping, ensemble of reward models) lack a principled stopping rule

bottleneck_statement: The user's RLHF pipeline exhibits the canonical reward-model overoptimization gap: the policy KL constraint to the SFT prior holds, but the proxy reward keeps climbing while a held-out gold reward plateaus and then degrades — Goodhart's law on a learned proxy. dblp:conf/iclr/MoskovitzSSSSDM24 (Constrained RLHF) reformulates this as constrained RL with a threshold on the proxy/true gap proxy, but chooses the threshold heuristically and does not derive a principled tightening schedule. dblp:conf/icml/ZhuJJ24 (Iterative Data Smoothing) attacks the upstream cause (reward model overfitting on sparse preferences) but the smoothing strength is empirically tuned. openalex:W4399115631 (SFT-as-adversarial regularizer) derives an asymptotic equivalence between SFT loss and an adversarial reward regularizer, opening a formal-reframing path, but does not characterize finite-sample behavior — the user's regime. The unaddressed gap: a derivable, finite-sample-valid stopping/regularization criterion tied to the proxy-true reward divergence, rather than a heuristic threshold or empirical smoothing strength.

closest_adjacent.what_they_did: Reformulates RLHF as a constrained RL problem, with a constraint on a proxy of the proxy-vs-true reward gap; uses Lagrangian methods to enforce the constraint during PPO training.
closest_adjacent.where_they_stopped: Constraint threshold and Lagrangian schedule are heuristic; no principled rule derived from the proxy-true gap structure for when/how to tighten.

closest_adjacent.what_they_did: Iterative data smoothing on the preference dataset to reduce reward-model overfitting upstream; theoretically motivated by overfit-bias decomposition.
closest_adjacent.where_they_stopped: Smoothing strength is iteratively tuned; no closed-form characterization of when smoothing prevents overoptimization vs when policy still exploits residual gap.

closest_adjacent.what_they_did: Derives an equivalence between SFT loss term and an adversarial regularizer on the reward model, providing a formal-reframing of why SFT-anchored RLHF mitigates overoptimization.
closest_adjacent.where_they_stopped: Equivalence is asymptotic in dataset size and policy capacity; finite-sample / finite-data behavior (the user's regime) not characterized — the regularization strength is derived only in the limit.

closest_adjacent.what_they_did: Trains an ensemble of reward models, uses ensemble disagreement as an uncertainty signal to penalize policy when proxy reward is high but uncertain.
closest_adjacent.where_they_stopped: Ensemble disagreement is a heuristic proxy for the true-vs-proxy reward gap; no derivation of when disagreement provably tracks the gap, so calibration is empirical.

what_phase_0_did_not_address: a finite-sample regularization schedule tied analytically to the proxy-true reward divergence (rather than heuristic thresholds, empirical smoothing strengths, or ensemble-disagreement proxies)
a derivable stopping rule for RLHF policy optimization that does not require access to a held-out gold reward model
characterization of when the SFT-as-adversarial-regularizer equivalence (W4399115631) breaks at finite data — and what to do at the breaking regime

domain_pattern_distribution.notes: assumption_audit_and_relax dominates this area (4/8 on-topic papers, primary or secondary). The 3 audit-class papers in closest_adjacent (Moskovitz, Zhu+audit, W4417212154+audit) share a common stopping point: heuristic / iterative / disagreement-based regularization schedules with no derived form. analytic_refinement and auxiliary_signal are at medium saturation; formal_reframing is under-saturated despite providing the only existing finite-sample-anchored theoretical equivalence (W4399115631).
